---
title: "DB_Check"
author: "KEC"
date: "2026-04-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
source("setup.R")
```

```{r read_WSD}

WSD <- read_csv("TEMP_DB.csv", na = c("U","NA"), skip = 0) 


# Create copy of source table as sf_object (i.e., with geospatial attributes). 
# During this process, it is required that rows without location data are
# dropped. Circle back to this later.
WSD_GEO <- WSD %>%
  dplyr::mutate(source_longitude = as.numeric(source_longitude),
                source_latitude = as.numeric(source_latitude)) %>%
  drop_na("source_longitude","source_latitude") %>%
  st_as_sf(., 
           coords = (c("source_longitude","source_latitude")), 
           crs = 4326,
           remove = FALSE) 

mapview(WSD_GEO)


mapview(select_sources)

```


```{r manual_update}
park <- 


```

```{r add_GWUDI}
# ONE TIME EDIT

#WSD_UPDATE <- WSD %>%
#  dplyr::mutate(gwudi = ifelse(grepl("GWUDI", system_type), "yes", "no"))
 

```


```{ r read_individual_updates}



```

```{r check_update}

identify_edits <- function(df_old, df_new, id_col = "wsd_source_id") {
  
  # 1. Clean and convert to character
  df_old_clean <- df_old %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
  df_new_clean <- df_new %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
  
  # 2. Check for duplicate IDs before proceeding
  dupes_old <- df_old_clean %>% count(!!sym(id_col)) %>% filter(n > 1)
  dupes_new <- df_new_clean %>% count(!!sym(id_col)) %>% filter(n > 1)
  
  if(nrow(dupes_old) > 0 | nrow(dupes_new) > 0) {
    warning("Duplicate IDs found! Check your ID column for non-unique values.")
    print(bind_rows(old = dupes_old, new = dupes_new, .id = "df"))
  }

  # 3. Pivot and Compare
  diffs <- bind_rows(old = df_old_clean, new = df_new_clean, .id = "source_version") %>%
    pivot_longer(-c(source_version, !!sym(id_col)), names_to = "column_name") %>%
    # values_fn ensures we don't get 'list-cols' if duplicates exist
    pivot_wider(names_from = source_version, 
                values_from = value, 
                values_fn = ~paste(unique(.x), collapse = "; ")) %>%
    # Handle NAs explicitly to avoid logic errors
    filter(
      (is.na(old) & !is.na(new)) | 
      (!is.na(old) & is.na(new)) | 
      (old != new)
    )

  # 4. Final Output
  if (nrow(diffs) > 0) {
    message(paste("❌ Found", nrow(diffs), "edits:"))
    diffs %>%
      mutate(edit_summary = paste0("ID [", !!sym(id_col), 
                                   "] | Col: ", column_name, 
                                   " | '", old, "' -> '", new, "'")) %>%
      pull(edit_summary) %>%
      walk(print)
  } else {
    message("✅ No differences found.")
  }
}
# Example Usage:
identify_edits(WSD, WSD_UPDATE, id_col = "wsd_source_id")



```




This function takes the existing "master" CSV and creates a timestamped copy in an archive folder.

This ensures that the main scripts always point to the same filename, while archiving past versions.


```{r write_update_and_archive}


library(tools) # For file_path_sans_ext

create_archive <- function(file_path = "TEMP_DB.csv", 
                           archive_dir = "data/Water_Supply_Systems/Copies and Backups") {
  
  # 1. Safety check: Does the source file actually exist?
  if (!file.exists(file_path)) {
    stop("Error: The source file '", file_path, "' was not found.")
  }
  
  # 2. Create the archive directory if it's missing
  if (!dir.exists(archive_dir)) {
    dir.create(archive_dir)
    message("Created directory: ", archive_dir)
  }
  
  # 3. Strip the path and extension to get the clean filename
  base_name <- file_path_sans_ext(basename(file_path))
  
  # 4. Generate timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM)
  # Adding time prevents errors if you run this twice in one day
  timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d_%H%M")
  
  # 5. Construct the new archived path
  archive_name <- paste0(base_name, "_", timestamp, ".csv")
  dest_path <- file.path(archive_dir, archive_name)
  
  # 6. Perform the copy
  file.copy(from = file_path, to = dest_path, overwrite = FALSE)
  
  message("✅ Archive created: ", dest_path)
}

# Example Usage:
create_archive()




```


"human-in-the-loop" safeguard for WSD. Requires manual confirmation of any edits to prevent accidental bulk-overwrites.


```{r write_update}


update_database <- function(current_db, new_db, 
                            id_col = "wsd_source_id",
                            target_file = "TEMP_DB.csv") {
  
  # 1. Print the Edits
  # This uses the robust identify_edits function we built earlier
  message("--- REVIEWING PROPOSED CHANGES ---")
  identify_edits(current_db, new_db, id_col = id_col)
  
  # 2. Ask for User Confirmation
  # The \n creates a new line for a cleaner look in the console
  user_confirm <- readline(prompt = "\nDo you want to proceed with these updates? (yes/no): ")
  
  # 3. Logic Gate
  if (tolower(user_confirm) %in% c("yes", "y")) {
    
    message("\nProceeding with update...")
    
    # 4. Create Archive
    # Using your updated function with default arguments
    create_archive()
    
    # 5. Write the New Database
    # Writing to your target file (e.g., WSD_Master.csv)
    write_csv(new_db, target_file)
    
    message("✅ Update Complete! Database written to: ", target_file)
    
  } else {
    # If the user types anything else, the function stops
    message("\n❌ Update cancelled by user. No changes made.")
  }
}

# Example Usage:
update_database(WSD, WSD_UPDATE)



```


```{r system_info}

system_cols <- c("park_name",
                 "region",
                 "state",
                 "park_unit",
                 "water_system_name",
                 "wsd_system_id", # Index
                 "alternative_name",
                 "classification",
                 "pws_number",
                 "fmss_number",
                 "fmss_system_name",
                 "description",
                 "point_of_use",
                 "water_entry_point_unique_id",
                 "active",
                 "seasonal",
                 "system_type",
                 "gwudi",
                 "treatment_system_type",
                 "use_category",
                 "storage_capacity_gallons",
                 "num_storage_tanks")

WSD_SYSTEM <- WSD %>%
  select(all_of(system_cols))



```
